danger_signals.visual_form: Japanese mythological monsters and creatures with exaggerated hostile features
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates combat encounters and threat presence in environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Dramatically enlarged creatures (e.g., giant harpy in Stage 4 JP version) with elaborate visual designs
danger_signals.meaning: Signals major threat escalation and stage-end danger threshold
Central narrative threat—the source of enemy corruption and world disturbance
World-state indicator: peaceful NPCs have been corrupted by Black Mantle's influence

safety_signals.visual_form: Traditional Shinto shrine maiden outfit with spiritual iconography
safety_signals.meaning: Player character represents protective, purifying spiritual authority; communicates player agency as cleanser/protector

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly talking raccoon creature with expressive features
safety_signals.meaning: Ally indicator; Japanese folklore benevolence; emotional anchor against overwhelming difficulty

safety_signals.visual_form: Traditional Japanese spiritual talismans and ritual implements as weapons
safety_signals.meaning: Player tools are sanctified/purifying; indicates spiritual combat against supernatural threats

safety_signals.visual_form: HUD hearts (or life bars in early build) and bomb icons
safety_signals.meaning: Resource abundance indicates safety margin; visual depletion signals danger escalation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shinto shrine iconography, ofuda talismans, gohei staffs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Shrine maiden outfit, spiritual purity aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All corrupted enemies and monsters serve under Black Mantle's influence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corruption of previously safe entities (goblins); stage bosses reflect Black Mantle's power

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse creature types across stages; some show corruption/control visual markers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appearance varies by stage environment; boss variants indicate stage-tier threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goblins as friendly, non-hostile entities alongside Rocky
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Goblins cooperate with player characters; no combat required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goblins transformed into enemies; monsters proliferate across stages; environmental darkness/hostility increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All entities attack player; stage progression blocked by enemy waves; boss encounters mandatory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss designs become more elaborate and visually powerful; Stage 4 harpy (JP) vs. armored bird knight (INT) shows visual power scaling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patterns intensify; boss attack frequency and complexity increase per stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage 4 boss redesigned from demonic harpy to armored bird knight in international versions; Stage 6 stained-glass faces anti-aliased to reduce demonic appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat difficulty parameters adjusted (lives/bombs differ by region); story presentation differs (European version removes cutscenes entirely)

environmental_storytelling.example: Different Japanese mythological creatures appear in specific stages, building visual narrative of spreading corruption; stage themes reflect yokai domains

Stage bosses grow in visual complexity and supernatural power; Stage 4 harpy→bird knight represents corruption intensity; final stage stained-glass imagery (demonic in JP, sanitized in INT) tells story of spiritual corruption severity
Pocky's outfit and weapon choice (ofuda talismans) establish her as spiritual purifier; visual design immediately communicates her role in dispelling corruption
Rocky's goblin companions transformed from friendly to hostile enemies; this single visual shift communicates the scale and invasiveness of Black Mantle's influence
Life bar/heart count and bomb counter depletion visually communicate world danger escalation; full resources = safety, depleted = crisis state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Offensive stance; fires ofuda projectiles; movement responsive to input
Follows player; provides dialogue/emotional support; no combat role (primarily narrative presence)
Shift from peaceful NPCs to hostile enemies; attack patterns indicate stage-specific corruption intensity
High-complexity attack patterns; arena-filling presence; visual emphasis on supernatural power; defeat triggers stage progression
Not directly encountered until endgame; presence inferred through corrupted minions; final confrontation triggers game conclusion

notes: Pocky & Rocky uses Japanese mythological visual language as core world-state vocabulary. The shrine maiden protagonist and tanuki companion establish spiritual/folk-tale aesthetic that positions player as purifier against supernatural corruption. Regional localization shows significant visual censoring (demonic imagery softened, harpy→bird knight redesign), indicating how visual communication of danger/evil was culturally contextualized. The overwhelming cuteness-versus-difficulty contrast mentioned in TCRF is itself a world-state signal: cute aesthetics mask catastrophic threat progression, creating cognitive dissonance that reflects player's 'overwhelming' challenge. Difficulty settings (Easy: 5 lives/3 bombs vs. Hard: 2 lives/0 bombs) alter perceived world-state safety through HUD resource visualization rather than environmental change.
